items.mistake: Using run name as the only identifier and reusing the same name every day
items.fix: Always record the system-generated run ID in logs and deployment metadata; treat names as human hints only.
items.why: Names collide; only IDs are guaranteed unique in the tracking backend.
items.example: Two runs named `baseline` on different dates are ambiguous; IDs `f3a1…` and `9c02…` are not.

items.mistake: Registering every run to the model registry
items.fix: Register only artifacts that passed validation; keep exploratory runs in experiment storage with tags.
items.why: Registry clutter makes Production promotion error-prone and hides the true candidate set.
items.example: After a sweep of 50 runs, register version 12 only for run ID `7b2e10` tagged `candidate` with best val_f1.

items.mistake: Comparing runs logged with different metric keys (`acc` vs `val_accuracy`)
items.fix: Standardize metric and param names in training code before the sweep starts.
items.why: Side-by-side UI columns stay empty or sort incorrectly when keys differ.
items.example: Run A logs `val_f1: 0.84`; Run B logs `f1_val: 0.86`—comparison tables may not align them as the same metric.

items.mistake: Replacing tags with notes in local files instead of on the run
items.fix: Set tags on the run in the tracking system so teammates and CI can filter programmatically.
items.why: Local README notes are invisible to comparison UIs and automation.
items.example: Add `mlflow.set_tag("data_version", "2024-06-01")` rather than only writing it in `notes.txt`.
